query: Glideo: Screen recordings that edit themselves review

results.title: Noodles beat NVIDIA | Product Hunt
results.content:
Mira — AI-moderated user interviews that watch how respondents feel while they answer, using facial coding, voice emotion, and eye tracking to flag the moments when what someone says doesn't match their face. From Entropik, nine years and 17 patents in human behavior research.

Glideo — Screen recordings that edit themselves. Record your demo or walkthrough and Glideo trims the pauses, adds captions, and removes filler — finished clip, no timeline. For demos, tutorials, and async updates where you want the result to look considered without spending an hour in an editor.

# Glideo 0.3.0: multi-clip recording, a facecam track, and precise overlays

by

Glideo

Glideo

•

Hi everyone,

Glideo 0.3.0 is out. It auto-updates in place, or grab it fresh at glideo.app. This release turns Glideo from a single-take recorder into a more robust multi-clip editor, while keeping the auto-zoom that does the editing for you.

What's new:

- Record more into one project. Re-record a video take onto any track, or narration onto any audio track, straight from that track's header. Each take counts in with a 3-2-1 countdown and starts right at the playhead, so it drops in exactly where you want it. [...] Report

Malani Willa

@nitesh\_kumar98 someone on my team has been looking for a lightweight screen recording editor. I think this could be a great fit, so I will send it their way.

Report

Simran Kumar

@nitesh\_kumar98 The snapping improvements caught my attention. Small editing refinements like that often make a tool feel much more polished

Report

Scarlett Massey

@nitesh\_kumar98 nice progress on this release. It feels like you focused on making everyday editing smoother instead of just adding flashy features, and I appreciate that.

Report

Aarav Pittman

Bababot

Bababot

This is a really solid update. Making the facecam its own track feels like one of those changes that seems small until you actually start editing. Nice work.

Report

Maali Baali

Serand

Serand [...] Plus polish: cancelling an export now stops cleanly, play resumes where you paused, and new recordings start with a gentler default zoom.

Still free for individuals, no watermark, runs on-device, notarized direct download for macOS 14+. I'd love to hear how the multi-clip flow feels. If it's useful to you and you'd like to chip in, there's a Ko-fi, but it's entirely optional!

Thank you all for the love and support for the product!

44 views

### Replies

Best

Nitesh Kumar

Congrats on the update. I really like that the facecam is now its own track because it gives much more flexibility during editing.

Report

Bryan Williamson

@nitesh\_kumar98 The on-device approach is a nice touch. I always prefer editing tools that keep my recordings local whenever possible

Report

# The Best SoftrAlternatives

The best Softralternatives areBubble, Glide, Retool, Airtable, and UI Bakery.

Bubble screenshot

Bubble

Bubble

4.8 ·

Choose Bubbleif...

 ✓you’re building a full-stack app, not portals
 ✓you need complex workflows and conditional onboarding
 ✓you want plugins and APIs for custom features

See details ↓Glide screenshot

Glide

Glide

4.9 ·

Choose Glideif...

 ✓your data already lives in spreadsheets
 ✓you want the fastest prototype feedback loop
 ✓you’re building simple internal apps and MVPs

See details ↓Retool screenshot

Retool

Retool

4.8 ·

Choose Retoolif... [...] 4.9 · 16 reviews

Learn more →

Glide is the fastest route from tabular data to a working app, which makes it a compelling alternative to Softr when speed of experimentation matters most. Its spreadsheet-native approach turns Sheets-style structures into functional mobile-friendly experiences with minimal configuration.

Glide shines when you’re iterating: adding fields, changing screens, and testing new flows can happen in minutes, which is ideal for MVPs, pilots, and internal tools. If your use case is a lightweight operational app or a prototype that needs quick user feedback, Glide often gets to “usable” faster than more portal-oriented builders. [...] The trade-off is depth: Glide is intentionally opinionated, and it’s not the best fit for deeply custom systems or very complex business logic. It’s the right choice when the goal is to validate an idea or operationalize a spreadsheet process, not build a highly bespoke web product.

### Best for

Ideal for teams that want to turn spreadsheet-like data into simple apps fast.

### Standout features

 ✓Spreadsheet-to-app workflow
 ✓Rapid iteration and prototyping loop
 ✓Mobile-friendly app layouts
 ✓Prebuilt components for common screens
 ✓Low friction setup for non-coders

## Retool

RetoolRetool

Build internal tools, remarkably fast
